texto: No boy was ever so interesting to them as when he was interesting to someone else.
explicacao: Citação de Anna-Marie McLemore sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “No boy was ever so interesting to them as when he was interesting to someone else.” articula reflexao, jealousy, interesting em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: ...both he and she were creek beds, quiet when they were full and quiet when they were dry. But when they were half-full, wearing a coat of shallow water, the current bumped over the rocks and valleys in the creek beds, wearing down the earth. Giving someone else a little of who they were hurt more than giving up none or all of it.

frase_original: ...both he and she were creek beds, quiet when they were full and quiet when they were dry. But when they were half-full, wearing a coat of shallow water, the current bumped over the rocks and valleys in the creek beds, wearing down the earth. Giving someone else a little of who they were hurt more than giving up none or all of it.

texto: They prickled her like thorns and leaves growing under her skin, and she felt the ache of a glass vine caging her forearm. They would crack, and the jagged pieces would cut into her wrists. Her blood would tint the glass. It would splinter and cut deeper into her.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anna-Marie McLemore: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “They prickled her like thorns and leaves growing under her skin, and she felt the ache of a glass…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, pain, would pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: They prickled her like thorns and leaves growing under her skin, and she felt the ache of a glass vine caging her forearm. They would crack, and the jagged pieces would cut into her wrists. Her blood would tint the glass. It would splinter and cut deeper into her.
